text: The Story of Saint Giong

speaker: Davis
text: Hello everyone, and welcome! I'm Davis, your storyteller, and I'm absolutely delighted to have you join me today.
text_tts: Hello, everyone, and welcome! I'm Davis, your storyteller, and I'm absolutely delighted to have you join me today.

speaker: Davis
text: Settle in, get comfortable, because we're about to embark on a thrilling journey into a captivating tale from Vietnamese folklore.
text_tts: Settle in, get comfortable, because we're about to embark on a THRIL-LING journey into a captivating tale, from Vietnamese folklore.

speaker: Davis
text: Today, we'll delve into the legend of "The Story of Saint Giong" – a heroic legend of strength, courage, and a most unusual childhood.
text_tts: Today, we'll delve into the legend of "The Story of Saint Giong" - a heroic legend of strength, courage, and a most unusual childhood.

speaker: Davis
text: Now, let me weave for you a tale, a legend from the heart of old Vietnam. It's the story of a most UNLIKELY hero, a boy named Giong.
text_tts: Now, let me weave for you a TALE... a LEGEND... from the heart of old Vietnam. It's the story of a most UNLIKELY hero... a boy named Giong.

speaker: Davis
text: Long, long ago, when Emperor Hung Vuong the Sixth ruled Vietnam, it was a time of peace and prosperity. The Red River flowed, teeming with boats, and the green rice fields stretched endlessly, shimmering under the sun. People were happy, industrious, and life was good.
text_tts: Long, LONG ago... when Emperor Hung Vuong the Sixth ruled Vietnam... it was a time of peace and prosperity. The Red River flowed... teeming with boats... and the GREEN rice fields stretched ENDLESSLY... shimmering under the sun. People were happy... industrious... and life was GOOD.

speaker: Davis
text: But alas, peace can be fragile. One day, enemies from the North, fierce and merciless, swept down upon the land. They burned towns, slaughtered innocents, and sowed terror wherever they went. The emperor's soldiers, brave as they were, simply could not hold them back.
text_tts: But alas... peace can be FRAGILE. One day... enemies from the North... FIERCE and MERCILESS... swept down upon the land. They burned towns... SLAUGHTERED innocents... and sowed TERROR wherever they went. The emperor's soldiers... brave as they were... simply could not hold them back.

speaker: Davis
text: In desperation, the emperor sent messengers far and wide, searching for anyone, ANYONE, strong enough to save the country. But hope dwindled with each passing day. No hero emerged.
text_tts: In desperation... the emperor sent messengers far and WIDE... searching for anyone... ANYONE... strong enough to save the country. But hope dwindled with each passing day. No hero emerged.

speaker: Davis
text: Now, in a small, quiet village called Phu Dong, there lived an old couple. They were kind, but their hearts carried a heavy sorrow. Their little boy, already three years old, still could not walk or speak. He was their joy, yet also their greatest heartache.
text_tts: Now... in a small, quiet village called Phu Dong... there lived an old couple. They were kind... but their hearts carried a HEAVY sorrow. Their little boy... already three years old... still could not walk... or speak. He was their joy... yet also their greatest heartache.

speaker: Davis
text: One day, a royal messenger, weary from his travels, arrived in Phu Dong. He shouted the emperor’s desperate plea, his voice echoing through the village. Suddenly, a miracle! The little boy, who had never uttered a sound, sat up and spoke clearly to his astonished parents.
text_tts: One day... a royal messenger... weary from his travels... arrived in Phu Dong. He shouted the emperor’s DESPERATE plea... his voice echoing through the village. SUDDENLY... a MIRACLE! The little boy... who had never uttered a sound... sat up... and spoke CLEARLY to his ASTONISHED parents.

speaker: Davis
text: He said, and I quote: “Please call the messenger. I want to help our country.” Imagine their shock! Barely daring to believe, they called the messenger over.
text_tts: He said, and I quote: “Please call the messenger. I want to help our country.” Imagine their SHOCK! Barely daring to believe... they called the messenger over.

speaker: Davis
text: The boy, with a newfound authority, instructed the messenger: “Go and tell the emperor I need an iron horse, iron armor, and an iron rod.” The messenger, though bewildered, wasted no time. He rushed back to the capital and relayed the boy's extraordinary request.
text_tts: The boy... with a NEWFOUND authority... instructed the messenger: “Go and tell the emperor... I need an iron horse... iron armor... and an iron rod.” The messenger... though bewildered... wasted NO time. He rushed back to the capital... and relayed the boy's EXTRAORDINARY request.

speaker: Davis
text: The emperor, desperate for any hope, immediately ordered his finest blacksmiths to forge these magnificent items. And while they toiled, a truly astonishing change began in the boy.
text_tts: The emperor... desperate for ANY hope... immediately ordered his finest blacksmiths to forge these MAGNIFICENT items. And while they toiled... a truly ASTONISHING change began in the boy.

speaker: Davis
text: Every day, he ate... and ate... and ATE. He devoured food in incredible quantities, growing bigger and taller with each passing hour, until he was no longer a boy, but a towering, powerful giant!
text_tts: Every day... he ate... and ate... and ATE. He devoured food in incredible quantities... growing BIGGER and TALLER with each passing hour... until he was no longer a boy... but a towering... POWERFUL giant!

speaker: Davis
text: Finally, the iron horse, armor, and rod arrived. The giant Giong, for that was his name now, stood up, donned the gleaming armor, and grasped the mighty iron rod. He mounted the iron horse, which thundered and roared, breathing fire from its nostrils! Inspired, the young men of the village, filled with awe, rallied behind him, ready to fight.
text_tts: FINALLY... the iron horse, armor, and rod arrived. The giant Giong... for that was his name NOW... stood up... donned the gleaming armor... and grasped the MIGHTY iron rod. He mounted the iron horse... which THUNDERED and ROARED... breathing FIRE from its nostrils! Inspired... the young men of the village... filled with awe... rallied behind him... ready to fight.

speaker: Davis
text: Giong rode fearlessly to the battlefield. The searing fire from his horse incinerated many enemies, and his iron rod struck down others again and again. The invaders, terrified, fled in disarray, leaving behind countless dead and wounded.
text_tts: Giong rode FEARLESSLY to the battlefield. The searing fire from his horse incinerated many enemies... and his iron rod struck down others AGAIN and AGAIN. The invaders... TERRIFIED... fled in disarray... leaving behind countless dead and wounded.

speaker: Davis
text: But the battle was not over. More enemies surged forward. Giong fought with unmatched bravery, but in the heat of battle, his iron rod shattered! Without hesitation, he tore up bamboo trees from the earth, using them as formidable weapons. He fought until he slew the last enemy general. The war was over. Vietnam was safe once more!
text_tts: But the battle was NOT over. More enemies surged forward. Giong fought with UNMATCHED bravery... but in the heat of battle... his iron rod SHATTERED! Without hesitation... he tore up bamboo trees from the earth... using them as FORMIDABLE weapons. He fought until he slew the last enemy general. The war was over. Vietnam was SAFE once more!

speaker: Davis
text: With the country saved, Giong instructed his loyal soldiers to return to the emperor with the good news. Then, he rode his magnificent iron horse up Soc Son Mountain. There, at the summit, he removed his armor, and with a final, majestic flourish, he ascended into the heavens, becoming, as people believed, a god.
text_tts: With the country saved... Giong instructed his loyal soldiers to return to the emperor with the good news. Then... he rode his MAGNIFICENT iron horse up Soc Son Mountain. There... at the summit... he removed his armor... and with a final... majestic flourish... he ASCENDED into the heavens... becoming... as people believed... a GOD.

speaker: Davis
text: To honor this extraordinary hero, the emperor built a grand temple and bestowed upon him the title, “Heavenly King of Phu Dong.” And to this very day, every year, the people of Vietnam celebrate the Saint Giong Festival, remembering the brave boy who grew into a giant and saved his homeland.
text_tts: To honor this extraordinary hero... the emperor built a grand temple... and bestowed upon him the title... “Heavenly King of Phu Dong.” And to this VERY day... every year... the people of Vietnam celebrate the Saint Giong Festival... remembering the brave boy who grew into a giant... and saved his homeland.

text: Outro

speaker: Davis
text: What a truly remarkable story of courage, sacrifice, and the unexpected hero!
text_tts: What a truly REMARKABLE story, of courage, sacrifice, and the unexpected HERO!

speaker: Davis
text: Saint Giong's legend reminds us that heroism can emerge from the most unlikely places, and that even the smallest among us can possess the power to change the world.
text_tts: Saint Giong's legend reminds us that heroism can emerge from the most unlikely places, and that even the SMALLEST among us can possess the power to change the world.

speaker: Davis
text: Thank you, from the bottom of my heart, for joining me on this journey into Vietnamese folklore.
text_tts: Thank you, from the bottom of my heart, for joining me on this journey into Vietnamese folklore.

speaker: Davis
text: If you enjoyed this tale, please consider subscribing and joining me next time for another captivating story from around our incredible world. Until then, farewell!
text_tts: If you enjoyed this tale, please consider subscribing, and joining me next time, for another CAPTIVATING story, from around our incredible world. Until then, farewell!
